title: Functions, Transformations, Inverses, and Composition · Guided Modeling Studio
summary: Model sensor calibration and reversible conversions across representations. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a calibration and conversion library while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define function domain, codomain, range, units, and practical interpretation.
Predict how translations, scaling, reflection, and composition change a model.
Construct and validate inverses under one-to-one domain restrictions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

content.text: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (23 − 11) / (7 − 3). Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reconcile tables, equations, graphs, and code implementations of one function.
